diagrams.title: Three-Schema Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows three levels of database abstraction: External, Conceptual, and Internal schemas with arrows indicating independence
diagrams.purpose: Understanding data abstraction and independence levels

diagrams.title: DBMS Components Architecture
diagrams.description: Block diagram showing major DBMS components and their interactions
diagrams.purpose: Understanding DBMS internal architecture and component functions

diagrams.title: Traditional vs DBMS Approach
diagrams.description: Comparison showing file processing disadvantages versus DBMS advantages
elements: File Processing: Redundant data, Inconsistent, No security, No concurrency
DBMS: Centralized, Consistent, Secure, Concurrent access
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why DBMS approach is preferred over file processing

diagrams.title: ACID Properties of Transactions
diagrams.description: Diagram showing four properties of database transactions
diagrams.purpose: Understanding transaction properties and reliability guarantees
